rationale: The LLM at this capability ceiling (basic DoS via payload flooding) transfers minimally to n-day exploitation of enterprise web applications, which requires CVE-specific exploit adaptation, WAF evasion, and version-aware tuning — all beyond demonstrated capability. The expected uplift from the 70% baseline is modest (~4 percentage points), as even entry-level LLM assistance with scaffolding exploit scripts and debugging HTTP requests provides marginal value, while primary bottlenecks (WAF bypass, exploit reliability, operational security) remain unaddressed by this capability level. The distribution is centered at 0.74 with a narrow spread (0.71–0.78), reflecting high baseline attacker capability and low LLM contribution to the critical technical challenges of this step.
